response.global_summary: The video features a virtual performer (VTuber) Kazama Iroha performing live on stage. She sings 'Non-breath oblige' (ノンブレス・オブリージュ) by PinocchioP in the first segment and 'ray' by BUMP OF CHICKEN in the second segment, with a talking interlude in between.

scenes.description: Kazama Iroha sings 'Non-breath oblige' on stage.
scenes.song_title: ノンブレス・オブリージュ

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
It's creepy to be liked by everyone in this world
But I want to hold hands with you in a classroom where we can't connect
I'll raise the white flag to the tyranny of the majority, suppress my ill feelings and say cheese
A sweet, post-truth french kiss - a warped, erotic love & peace
A self-centered ray gun, firing wildly the nonsense of the strong
An haute couture license to kill
A game of musical chairs that created division, a baby that is given birth by epidural labor
We're calling a grandiose in-joke "history"
I want to live but I'm told to die, I want to die but I'm told to live
I want to live but I'm told to die, I want to die but I'm told to live
Is it wrong to boast of my happiness? Is it wrong to grieve over my unhappiness?
I'm avoiding impudent words for our glorious future
One, two
"There are many people in this world who can't even breathe"
One, two
"There are many people in this world who can't even breathe"
If I'm true to one side, I'm false to the other; we give, we take, we protect, we misunderstand
I stop in the minefield (and obediently become a dog, woof)
Non-breath, non-breath, non-breath, non-breath oblige
I love you
Under the surface of my suffocating days, a flower of blood smokes and flickers
We use our
complexes, complexes, complexes, complexes as weapons to fight
For the sake of our convenience and freedom
We obligate ourselves to hold our breath
I can't breathe, I can't breathe, I can't breathe, I can't breathe,

I'm afraid of decent people who wield chainsaws
Saying it's self-defense; brilliant love, brilliant spring
Important things are slowly disappearing each and every day

Sympathy, envy, jealousy, resentment, black tears falling down
My ugly emotions burst out and become a snow-white bird

I love you
Swimming with deep-sea fish below the freezing point, looking up at the stars of granulated sugar
We don't
Directly, directly, directly, directly do anything in person

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
I now feel like the day we said goodbye is further away than it really is;
I’ve worn my heels down sealing away that sorrowful light.
The things I saw when I was with you are invisible to me now;
All that’s left is a ghost-like comet that’s become the only thing I search for.
I sang a song with a melody made up of that time with you,
But it wasn’t sad at all, since I now know what sadness really is.
I don’t even have time to think about “when” or “how far”,
Normal or abnormal. Just moving forward is hard enough.
Life is better when you’re having fun, so I’ll pretend like we’re always smiling.
Don’t worry; even if I forget this pain, it will never fade away.
Reality is beginning to overwrite the road I paved with ideals,
And my memories are left glittering upon the path I’ve taken.
Why did we part ways? What were we trying to accomplish?
That sorrowful light is extending my shadow far beyond where I stand.
Sometimes I catch a fever and catch sleep where I can;
Once I wake up in my dream, I’ll set off with you again.
Fair weather is far off, especially in this unending darkness,
But when we think of the stars, we’ll find ourselves out in the galaxy.
Even if we stop our tears and change into new shoes,
Don’t worry; even if I forget this pain, it will never fade away.
Surely I had something I wanted to tell you,
But it was probably really cliché.
The reason we parted ways must be related to the reason we met,
Just like that ghost-like comet; ethereal and unending.
I think it’s best to live your life not having time,
For everyone else’s “yes”, “no”, and “maybe”.
Life is better when you’re having fun, so we’ll pretend like we’re always smiling.
Don’t worry; even if I forget this pain, it will never fade away.
Don’t worry; you’re there at the source of this light.

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing the VTuber Performance**

Okay, so I've been tasked with dissecting this video of a VTuber's singing performance. My goal is clear: I need to identify the musical segments, determine the songs being performed, and then retrieve the lyrics, both in the original language (Japanese) and in English. I'll need to package all of this up into a neatly structured JSON output.

First, I've mapped out the video's segments. From 0:00 to 2:29, the VTuber is singing "ノンブレス・オブリージュ" (Non-Breath Oblige), that's easy enough. Then, there's a conversational interlude from 2:29 to 3:02—that doesn't need to be analyzed for songs. Finally, from 3:02 to 5:01, she sings another song, "ray".

I have the metadata now, as well. For "ノンブレス・オブリージュ," the original artist is PinocchioP (ピノキオピー), and the performer is Kazama Iroha (風真いろは). The song is in Japanese. For "ray," the original artist is BUMP OF CHICKEN, and again, Kazama Iroha is performing. The composer is 藤原基央 (Fujiwara Motoo), which I verified from the on-screen text.

Now, I'm formulating my search queries. I need to get the original and English lyrics for each song. For "ノンブレス・オブリージュ," I'll search for "ノンブレス・オブリージュ ピノキオピー 歌詞" for the original lyrics and "Non-Breath Oblige PinocchioP lyrics English" for the English translation. For "ray," I will search for "ray BUMP OF CHICKEN 歌詞" and "ray BUMP OF CHICKEN lyrics English." I'll refine the title for each song: "ノンブレス・オブリージュ" and "ray".

My plan is to use the `web_search_langsearch_multi` tool to execute these searches in parallel, then parse the results. Finally, I'll populate a `final_result` structure with the video's segment mapping, and the retrieved lyrics. Time to get to work and find these lyrics!



**Ready to Finalize the JSON Response**

Okay, I've gathered everything. I've successfully located both the original Japanese lyrics and English translations for both songs. For "ノンブレス・オブリージュ," the original lyrics are sourced from `uta-net`, a generally reliable source in this domain, and the English translation is from `lyricstranslate`. I've checked these for accuracy and completeness. Then, for "ray," the Japanese lyrics were retrieved from `lyrical-nonsense`, which I've found to be quite comprehensive and accurate. The English translation came from `pesni.guru`, which appears to provide full-text transcriptions and translations, so it should be dependable. With all the necessary information now at my disposal, I can proceed with constructing the final JSON response, ensuring that the data is organized correctly and complete. I think I have everything needed now to output the JSON data.
